free_text: Spallation of aluminum by 300 GeV protons
The proton intensity varied from 10**11 to 10**13
protons per pulse during the course of these
experiments, with a pulse repetition rate of ~8 s.
Three types of target arrangements were used. In the
first, the targets consisted of three or four
aluminum foils and three polyethylene foils. The
middle one or two aluminum foils were 14 mg/cm2 thick
and were guarded by 1.8 mg/cm2 aluminum foils; the
polyethylene foils were all 12 mg/cm2 thick. In the
second arrangement, graphite foils of thickness 23
mg/cm2 were substituted for the polyethylene. The
third target consisted only of aluminum foils; this
was used to measure the cross sections for production
of 7Be and 22Na relative to that for 22Na, with
The measurements were made by exposing a
stack of target and guard foils in the external
proton beam in the Meson Hall of the Fermilab
The compounding of the standard deviations
from the decay curve analyses and the estimated
uncertainties in absolute counting efficiencies. The
errors in the cross section values include the error
